DECLARATION OF ROBERT DE VRIES IN OPPOS TO DEFTS' SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE (CCP 425.16) FILED BY PLAINTIFF GIRARD, JAMES G

LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. DEFENDANTS AMEY NUTTER, TODD ROBINS, DELPHINE RENARD, CHRISTOPHER NUTTER AND TIMOTHY ZAMBELLI'S MOTION TO STRIKE (CCP 425.16) IS CONTINUED TO AUGUST 12, 2008 PER AGREEMENT OF THE PARTIES. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

DECLARATION OF D.I. BLUMENFELD IN SUPPORT OF SPECIAL MTN TO STRIKE FILED BY DEFENDANT ZAMBELLI, TIMOTHY NUTTER, AMEY ROBINS, TODD ERIC (ERRONEOUSLY SUED HEREIN AS TODD ERIC ROBBINS) RENARD, DELPHINE CHARLOTTE NUTTER, CHRISTOPHER

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS AMEY NUTTER, TODD ROBINS, DELPHINE RENARD, CHRISTOPHER NUTTER AND TIMOTHY ZAMBELLI'S MOTION TO STRIKE (CCP 425.16) IS DENIED. ONLY THE FIRST TWO CAUSES OF ACTION IMPLICATE THE ANTI-SLAPP STATUTE. PLAINTIFF HAS PROVIDED SUFFICIENT EVIDENCE TO ESTABLISH A PRIMA FACIE CASE. THE DISMISSAL IS A FAVORABLE TERMINATION; THE LAWYER'S ADVISE IS NOT DETERMINATIVE GIVEN THE FACTS RELATED TO PLAINTIFF'S NOTICE OF A DISABILITY AND THE CONTENT OF THE BLUMEFELD MEMO; AND THE FACTS SET FORTH IN PARAGRAPH 23 OF GIRARD'S DECLARATION PROVIDE A BASIS FOR FINDING MALICE. DEFENDANT FAILS TO DEMONSTRATE THAT THE REMAINING CAUSES OF ACTION ARE COVERED BY CCP 425.16. THE GRAVAMAN OF THOSE CLAIMS EMBRACE NON-LITIGATION CONDUCT. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. ORDER ON DEFENDANT'S EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

Notice Of Defts' Motion To Strike (Ccp 425.16); Request For Judicial Notice In Support Of Defts' Special Motion To Strike
Off Calendar Aug-12-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Nils Rosenquest of Rosenquest and Associates (292.0980) and J. Wallace Oman (658.5400) for plaintiff and responding party
Charles Stone of Roger Scott & Helmer (650.365.7700) for defendants and moving parties

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANTS AMEY NUTTER, TODD ROBINS, DELPHINE RENARD, CHRISTOPHER NUTTER AND TIMOTHY ZAMBELLI'S MOTION TO STRIKE (CCP 425.16) is denied. Only the first two causes of action implicate the anti-SLAPP statute. Plaintiff has provided sufficient evidence to establish a prima facie case. The dismissal is a favorable termination; the lawyer's advise is not determinative given the facts related to plaintiff's notice of a disability and the content of the Blumefeld memo; and the facts set forth in paragraph 23 of Girard's declaration provide a basis for finding malice. Defendant fails to demonstrate that the remaining causes of action are covered by CCP 425.16. The gravaman of those claims embrace non-litigation conduct. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. Order on defendant's evidentiary objections signed in open Court. (302/PJM/PB) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600
